### Introduction to PWM
Pulse Width Modulation (PWM) is a technique used to encode a message into a pulsing signal. It is commonly used in power electronics, telecommunications, and other fields where signal processing is crucial.

### Definition and Principle
PWM is a method of controlling the average power delivered to a load by switching the power devices (such as transistors or thyristors) on and off at a high frequency. The average value of the voltage (or current) fed to the load is controlled by varying the duty cycle of the switch. The duty cycle is the ratio of the time the switch is on to the total time of the switching period.
